suggestions: doc_md says the catalog is "exposed as two DuckDB table functions" but the catalog also exposes the `content_collections` view; add a third bullet for it and point agents there as the credential-free entry point, since it is the only object queryable without an `azure_graph` secret.

The comment pins v1 at "Sites.Selected (opt-in Sites.Read.All for fleet scans)" while doc_llm lists "Sites.Selected, Sites.Read.All, or Files.Read.All" — reconcile these, and state which permission each function needs (Files.Read.All is a OneDrive/`drive_items` concern, not a SharePoint list one).

Neither doc mentions deltaLink expiry. Graph returns 410 Gone / `resyncRequired` on a stale or too-old token; document what the function does in that case (throw? silently full-resync?) because an agent holding a `{resourceId -> deltaLink}` map must know whether to catch-and-reset.
ed1445ef52eeec52.summary: Strong framing of the multi-cursor delta model, but it undercounts its own surface and leaves cursor-expiry behavior undefined.

suggestions: doc_llm claims "the $select / $expand / $top projection is pinned for the life of a token," but per `src/functions.ts` these apply only to the initial-sync URL and `page_size` ($top) is explicitly best-effort — describing $top as "pinned" is misleading. Say instead: $select/$expand are baked into the server's deltaLink and cannot be changed without a full resync; $top is a per-request hint Graph may ignore.

Add the shared output contract once here rather than leaving it implicit: both functions return the business columns `id, change_type, removed_reason, name, size, web_url, last_modified_date_time` plus control columns `_row_kind` / `_delta_next` last, and the marker row has ALL business columns NULL. `removed_reason` is documented nowhere in any object under review.

State that exactly one marker row is emitted per scan and that it is always emitted (even when zero items changed), so an agent can unconditionally read `_delta_next` and knows an empty incremental scan is a 1-row, not 0-row, result.
9ad59f7d08b12577.summary: Clear and well-shaped, but overstates $top as pinned and omits the shared column contract including `removed_reason`.

suggestions: The `collection` slug values (`drive_items` / `list_items`) do not both match the `table_function` values (`drive_items` / `sharepoint_list_items`). Call this out in the `collection` column comment so an agent doesn't build a function name by string-templating the slug.

For a discovery view whose whole purpose is routing an agent to the right call, add a `required_parameters` column (e.g. `drive` vs `site,list`) — otherwise the agent must leave this view and read each function's signature to learn that one takes a single id and the other takes two.

The examples only project columns; add one that closes the loop, e.g. a comment showing the discovered `table_function` value being called: `-- SELECT table_function FROM ... WHERE collection='drive_items'; then: SELECT * FROM azure.main.drive_items('<driveId>') WHERE _row_kind IS NULL`.
0f34d01e8f14802f.summary: Accurate and appropriately scoped as a credential-free index, but stops one column short of actually routing an agent to a callable invocation.

suggestions: The `expand` parameter description omits the pinning caveat that the sibling `drive_items.select` description carries. Since `$expand` is baked into the returned deltaLink, changing `expand` on a subsequent call while passing an old `delta_token` silently has no effect — state this explicitly, as it is a real footgun for an agent tuning the projection.

Example 1 selects `web_url` and `last_modified_date_time`, and the schema also emits `size` and `removed_reason`, yet the prose describes none of these for listItems. Document that listItems share the `driveItems` column set, that `size` is always NULL for list items (they are metadata, not bytes), and that `removed_reason` is populated only on `change_type = 'removed'` rows.

doc_llm says "the item Title surfaces as the `name` column" — true only under the default `expand := 'fields'`. Note that overriding `expand` to anything not including `fields` yields NULL `name`, so an agent that customizes the projection knows it is trading away that mapping.
f8b5d901a6d55b59.summary: Cursor semantics are documented well; the column set, the always-NULL `size`, `removed_reason`, and `expand`'s pinning are all missing.

suggestions: The documented `parameters` order is wrong. It lists drive, delta_token, include_download_url, page_size, select, but `parameter_types` is [VARCHAR, VARCHAR, VARCHAR, BIGINT, BOOLEAN] and `src/functions.ts:72` declares the true order as `drive, delta_token, select, page_size, include_download_url`. An agent that trusts the prose and calls positionally will pass a boolean into `select`. Fix the docs to match the registration order (or state that these are named-only parameters).

doc_llm lists "Columns: name, size, web_url, last_modified_date_time" — this omits `id` (present in every example), `change_type`, and `removed_reason` entirely, and gives no types or units. Say `size` is BIGINT bytes and NULL for folders/tombstones, and give the format of `last_modified_date_time`.

`_download_url` is called "short-lived" three times with no duration. State the actual TTL (Graph pre-authed URLs are ~1 hour) and pair the existing SECURITY warning with the concrete consequence: the URL grants unauthenticated read of the file's bytes to any bearer of the string, so it must not be persisted alongside the delta cursor.
7988326978594de7.summary: Documented parameter order contradicts both `parameter_types` and the source, making positional calls actively unsafe; column and TTL details are also thin.
